steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what the text most strongly suggests about Demps's paintings. The passage states that Demps imitated Hair's fast painting technique, which produces the impressionistic qualities 'synonymous with the group's shared aesthetic.' McLendon, by contrast, painted with greater attention to detail — implying his work is less typical of the Highwaymen style.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Demps's works are more aesthetically interesting than McLendon's: The passage makes no judgment about aesthetic interest or quality — it only discusses technique and typicality. ✗
  • Choice B — Demps's works are less derivative of Hair than typically acknowledged: The passage says Demps imitated Hair's technique, suggesting his works are derivative, not less so. ✗
  • Choice C — lack of precision makes them inferior to works by Hair or McLendon: The passage never evaluates quality or ranks the artists — this is an unsupported value judgment. ✗
  • Choice D — they likely have visual qualities regarded as more typical of Highwaymen paintings than McLendon's: Demps used the fast painting technique that defines the group's aesthetic; McLendon did not. This directly follows from the passage. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
